options.text: It’s completely destroying teamwork because people never meet face-to-face.
It’s reshaping teamwork—not replacing it. The key is intentional design.
Teamwork is easier now because everyone works alone.
I don’t think technology matters much for remote teams.

content.explanation: 正确答案使用了 'reshaping—not replacing' 这一精准表达，准确回应了对方关于团队合作是否被摧毁的担忧，同时引入 'intentional design' 这一关键概念。干扰项1夸大了负面影响，干扰项3错误地认为团队合作等于面对面互动，干扰项4忽略了技术能力的重要性，均不符合对话中积极、建设性的共识构建语境。
content.speakerText: So, to wrap up—remote work demands better tech skills and cultural awareness, but it’s not killing teamwork, right?
content.speakerTextCn: 总结一下——远程工作要求更好的技术能力和文化意识，但没毁掉团队合作，对吧？

options.text: Actually, I think flexibility hurts productivity more than it helps.
I couldn’t agree more. Clear guidelines make flexibility sustainable.
I’m not sure—I think we should just let everyone do whatever they want.
We didn’t discuss communication at all, so this isn’t agreed upon.

content.explanation: 正确答案使用了 'I couldn’t agree more' 表达强烈认同，并补充了 'sustainable' 来深化共识，符合总结共识的语境。干扰项1否定共识，干扰项3缺乏建设性，干扰项4否认已有讨论，均不符合达成共识的对话目标。
content.speakerText: Let’s summarize what we’ve agreed on so far: flexibility improves productivity, but we need clear communication guidelines. Does that sound right?
content.speakerTextCn: 让我们总结一下到目前为止我们达成的共识：灵活性提高生产力，但我们需要清晰的沟通准则。这样对吗？

options.text: I think tools are everything—trust is just a bonus.
Exactly. Tools enable collaboration, but trust makes it meaningful.
I don’t use any tools, so I can’t comment.
We should stop using tools and focus only on personal relationships.

content.explanation: 正确答案用 'enable' 和 'makes it meaningful' 精准区分了工具与信任的功能，体现了对复杂共识的深刻理解。干扰项1贬低信任，干扰项3回避讨论，干扰项4极端化观点，均未体现平衡与共识构建。
content.speakerText: To wrap up our discussion: collaboration tools are essential, but they’re not enough without trust. What do you think?
content.speakerTextCn: 总结我们的讨论：协作工具是必需的，但没有信任它们还不够。你怎么看？
